description_long: This hammer features a robust, industrial design with a long, cylindrical handle that appears to be made of wood or a wood-like material, capped with a black, rubberized grip at the base. The head is complex, with a black, angular striking face and two cylindrical yellow components mounted on top, possibly simulating a powered or mechanical function. Several colored wires (red, blue, and white) are visible, suggesting an electronic or pneumatic enhancement, and the head is attached to a hexagonal mounting plate. The handle is reinforced with a black, ribbed section near the head, likely for shock absorption. The overall construction combines both traditional and modern elements, giving it a high-tech, utility-focused appearance. The hammer is visually striking, with a mix of matte and slightly glossy finishes, and no visible signs of wear or branding.
description: A high-tech hammer with a wooden handle, black rubberized grip, and a complex, angular head featuring yellow cylinders and colored wires. The design blends traditional and modern elements for a utility-focused look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the yellow cylinders, colored wires, and the angular black head with the hexagonal mounting plate.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow profile of the head, the alignment of the yellow cylinders, and the central striking face.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the wires and the ribbed shock-absorbing section.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the handle's length, the black grip, and the symmetrical arrangement of the head components.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a black rubberized, textured grip at the base and a ribbed shock-absorbing section near the head.
attribute_head_type: Angular, industrial-style head with a flat striking face, two cylindrical yellow attachments on top, and a hexagonal mounting plate; no claw or peen visible.
attribute_attachment_or_accessory: Colored wires and two cylindrical yellow attachments integrated into the head
